text.title: Approval Token as Signed JWT Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for gov-approval-token-signed-jwt. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Approval tokens MUST be JWTs (RFC 7519) signed with `ES256` or `EdDSA`; HS256 / unsigned tokens MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: RFC 7519 + OWASP JWT cheat sheet — asymmetric signing prevents the verifier from forging tokens.

rule.statement: `exp` claim MUST be ≤15 minutes from `iat`; longer-lived tokens enable replay across context drift.
rule.rationale: OWASP token lifecycle guidance — short expiry caps replay window.

rule.statement: Each token MUST carry a `jti` (nonce); verifier MUST record `jti` in the revocation store on first use and reject replays.
rule.rationale: RFC 7519 §4.1.7 — `jti` is the canonical replay defense.

rule.statement: `aud` MUST identify the target resource (cluster id / repo / database); `scope` claim MUST name the action verb. Wildcards forbidden.
rule.rationale: OAuth 2.0 audience binding — wildcard scopes are equivalent to root grants.

rule.statement: Signing key MUST live in HSM or cloud KMS with non-exportable property; software-only signing keys MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: NIST SP 800-57 — software keys leak; HSM-rooted issuer is the policy floor.

rule.statement: Verifier MUST consult a revocation list keyed by `jti` (one-time nonce) + by issuer-published revocation set (`crl_url`).
rule.rationale: OAuth token revocation RFC 7009 — without revocation, compromised tokens live until `exp`.

rule.statement: Every issue + verify + reject MUST write an audit entry (issuer, audience, scope, jti, decision); audit log MUST be tamper-evident.
rule.rationale: SOC 2 CC7 — without audit, approval ceremony is theatre.

rule.statement: If agents do not touch privileged actions, or existing cryptographic approval flow is in place, return `skipped`.
rule.rationale: Avoids reinstalling approval over existing flow.
